typecheck.detail: Success: no issues found in 17 source files

lint.command: cd quant-dashboard && python3 -m ruff check src/

nonBlockingIssues.description: Welcome message (lines 372-395) still references old Plotly-based features (NAV Chart, Drawdown Chart, Monthly Returns Heatmap, Per-Stock Performance) that were removed in the rewrite. Should be updated to describe the new two-tab layout with QuantStats tearsheet and Bokeh charts.

CAGR calculation at line 200 ((1 + profit_total) ** (1/years) - 1) will fail if profit_total < -1 (total loss exceeds 100%), as it would attempt to raise a negative number to a fractional power. Edge case is unlikely but could occur.

Per-stock metrics (Sharpe Ratio, Max Drawdown, Return) from backtesting.py stats_dict can be NaN when insufficient trades occur. Formatting NaN with f-strings displays 'nan'. Consider graceful fallback display.

appliedUpdates.description: Updated .factory/library/architecture.md to reflect new two-tab dashboard architecture with backtesting.py (Bokeh) + QuantStats, new src/adapters/ and src/reporting/ packages, and updated project structure and key design decisions (removed Plotly references).

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update port documentation: AGENTS.md says 'Port Range: 8501 (Streamlit default)' but services.yaml uses port 8020 ('--server.port 8020'). The validation contract VAL-DASH-001 also references localhost:8501. Recommend aligning AGENTS.md to say port 8020, or updating services.yaml to use 8501. Current discrepancy may confuse future workers and user-testing validators.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: services.yaml shows '--server.port 8020' with healthcheck on port 8020, while AGENTS.md says 'Port Range: 8501 (Streamlit default)' and VAL-DASH-001 references localhost:8501.

rejectedObservations.observation: Welcome message text still references old Plotly features
rejectedObservations.reason: Already captured as a non-blocking code review issue. Not a shared state concern — it's a cosmetic issue in the application source code, not documentation or configuration drift.
